title: Protazk

{{Quote|Another dirt-ball world. Hey, sarge! How come we get all the good jobs?|Gortz, to [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Arbmab/Legends|Arbmab]]|<ref name="ISB" />}}
'''Protazk''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="ISB" /> located in the [[Protazk system/Legends|Protazk system]], a part of the [[Kwymar sector/Legends|Kwymar sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC Protazk" /> It was the site of [[Spuma Flats]],<ref name="ISB2" /> a region that shared its name with the [[Seswenna sector/Legends|Seswenna sector]]<ref name="EAOC Spuma">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Spuma system/Legends|Spuma system]]</ref> planet [[Spuma]].<ref name="CSWE Spuma">{{CSWECite|III|184|Spuma}}</ref> The terrain there included a flat, dusty plain on which [[Laser/Legends|laser]] emplacement towers and trenches were at one point established.<ref name="ISB" />

As part of the [[Kwymar Suppressions/Legends|Kwymar Suppressions]] campaign<ref name="THW1">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/huntwithin1|text=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 1|int=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 1}}</ref> that took place by [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]],<ref name="17 BBY">Per {{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/huntwithin1|text=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 1|int=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 1}}, the [[Assault on Protazk]] was part of the [[Kwymar Suppressions/Legends|Kwymar Suppressions]], which "Valance's Tale: Part 1" and {{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-11-serving-the-empire|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 11: Serving the Empire}} establish took place by [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] carried out a ruthless [[Assault on Protazk|assault]] on Protazk<ref name="THW1" /> in order to suppress an insurgency.<ref name="CSWE Protazk">{{CSWECite|III|47|Protazk}}</ref> At one point in the engagement, a [[Landing barge/Legends|landing barge]] deployed the [[S-1 FireHawke Heavy Repulsortank|repulsortank]] [[Hammer 3:2]]<ref name="ISB" /> of the [[Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit/Legends|Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit]]<ref name="CSWE Protazk" /> onto a plain of<ref name="ISB" /> Spuma Flats<ref name="ISB2" /> where the insurgents had taken up defensive positions. During the [[Hovertank/Legends|tank's]] deployment, its [[Vehicle Command Crewer|driver]] [[Gortz]] looked out the [[Tank/Legends|vehicle's]] [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] and cursed, characterizing Protazk as "another dirt-ball world." Hammer 3:2 then joined other repulsortanks of [[Squad three]] in advancing on the trenches.<ref name="ISB" />

==Behind the scenes==
The name "Protazk" was introduced in the context of a mention of a battle in the [[Star Wars Miniatures Battles (first edition)|first edition core rulebook]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars Miniatures Battles]]'' tabletop miniature wargame. The rulebook was written by [[Stephen Crane]] and [[Paul Murphy]]<ref name="MiniaturesBattles">[[Star Wars Miniatures Battles (first edition)|''Star Wars Miniatures Battles'', First Edition]]</ref> and was published in [[June]] [[1989]].<ref name="YearByYear">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> "[[The Assault on Protazk]]," a short companion narrative included in [[Greg Gorden]]'s [[October]] 1989 ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' that was published by West End Games for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', subsequently established that Protazk was a distinct world, with the narrative also featuring its first full appearance.<ref name="ISB" />

[[File:Fakir-Voyages13.png|thumb|right|200px|Map from ''Voyages SF'' 13 depicting Protazk as part of the Fakir sector.]]
"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed Protazk in the [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> [[Jason Fry]]'s [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Miniatures|''Star Wars'' Miniatures]] scenario series ''[[The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale]]'', published on [[Wizards.com]] between [[December 2]], [[2004]]<ref name="THW1" /> and [[January 27]], [[2005]],<ref name="THWC">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/huntwithin5|text=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Conclusion|int=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Conclusion}}</ref> then instead placed the world in the Kwymar [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="THW1" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Protazk [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Protazk itself, in grid square Q-4,<ref name="TEA" /> and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book later confirmed the placement from ''The Hunt Within''.<ref name="EAOC Protazk" />
